ieee_software.name: IEEE Software
ieee_software.scope: Software engineering methods, tools, processes, and practice. Bridges research and professional practice. Practitioner-focused.
ieee_software.audience: Working software engineers and engineering managers
ieee_software.word_limit_notes: Each figure or table counts as 250 words against the limit
ieee_software.template: Word or LaTeX; IEEE magazine style
ieee_software.special_notes: Three practitioner takeaways must be actionable, not restatements of abstract. Author photo required.

cacm_practice.name: Communications of the ACM — Practice
cacm_practice.scope: Engineering experience and practitioner knowledge from real-world CS and software practice. Not a research venue — engineering stories, case studies, and hard-won lessons.
cacm_practice.audience: Broad CS audience including non-SE specialists; practitioners and researchers
cacm_practice.tone: Accessible to non-specialists; must be followable by a computer scientist outside the subfield
cacm_practice.word_limit_notes: Approximately 6,000 words; 10 single-column single-spaced pages including figures
cacm_practice.reference_order: alphabetical by first author last name
cacm_practice.citation_style: ACM style; numbered or author-year depending on template; alphabetical in reference list
cacm_practice.special_notes: Key CACM test: could a computer scientist outside this subfield follow this article? Broad relevance is mandatory. Relevance statement required at submission.

cacm_research.name: Communications of the ACM — Research and Advances
cacm_research.scope: Innovative ideas, technical visions, open challenges, and significant results of broad interest to computing. Not a journal — results must be compelling to a broad CS audience.
cacm_research.audience: All of computing; broad CS readership including non-specialists
cacm_research.reference_order: alphabetical by first author last name
cacm_research.special_notes: Higher novelty bar than Practice section. Still requires broad accessibility. The 'why does this matter to all of CS?' test applies.

cacm_viewpoints.name: Communications of the ACM — Viewpoints / Opinion
cacm_viewpoints.scope: Broad-interest opinions, nontechnical perspectives, point-counterpoint debates on computing issues. Includes Viewpoints, Point/Counterpoint, and Letters.
cacm_viewpoints.audience: All of computing; opinion accessible to non-specialists
cacm_viewpoints.special_notes: Strongest thesis wins. CACM values point-counterpoint and well-argued contrarian views. The author must be willing to take a clear stance.

ieee_tse.name: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
ieee_tse.scope: Rigorous software engineering research: theoretical, empirical, methodological. All aspects of software development, testing, maintenance, and management.
ieee_tse.page_limit_format: IEEE two-column formatted pages (roughly 8,000-10,000 words equivalent)
ieee_tse.abstract_requirements: Structured: Objective, Methods, Results, Conclusion; self-contained; no abbreviations or citations
ieee_tse.template: IEEE two-column Transactions format; LaTeX preferred, Word acceptable
ieee_tse.open_access_apc_note: Hybrid journal; choose open access with APC or traditional
ieee_tse.special_notes: Threats to validity section is expected for empirical work. ORCID numbers for all authors strongly recommended. Replication package / data availability statement improves reception.

acm_queue.name: ACM Queue
acm_queue.scope: Deep technical practitioner content: what is hard about X in production? Distributed systems, performance, security, reliability, infrastructure. Written by engineers for engineers.
acm_queue.audience: Senior engineers, architects, tech leads; highly technical practitioners
acm_queue.tone: Conversational, direct, practitioner war-story style. No hedging. No academic formality.
acm_queue.special_notes: INVITATION ONLY. Cannot cold-submit. Must pitch to queue-info@acm.org or receive editorial invitation. The core editorial mandate: 'What is hard about implementing X? What are the gotchas?' — not promotional content. Articles not indexed as peer-reviewed; consider this for academic CV purposes.
